killReason: Interesting MLLM training research but too deep in ML infrastructure (attention reweighting, policy optimization) to qualify as creative-relevant AI — no meaningful connection to image, video, design, or generation work a creative would care about.
summary: DyCo-RL is a reinforcement learning method that improves visual reasoning in multimodal LLMs by dynamically coordinating attention between visual and textual tokens during chain-of-thought generation.

killReason: Solid VLM efficiency research but too infrastructure-focused — token routing optimization for model inference is off-domain for working creatives and has no direct creative application
summary: A training-free plug-in that replaces irreversible visual token pruning in vision-language models with recoverable routing, improving grounding accuracy under aggressive token reduction without changing compute budgets.

killReason: off-domain — pure ML architecture research with no connection to image, video, design, or any creative-relevant application
summary: An academic comparison of three subquadratic transformer alternatives (xLSTM, Mamba-2, GatedDeltaNet) across code and time-series tasks, finding xLSTM performs best due to its gating and memory correction scheme.

killReason: pure ML training infrastructure, zero creative-domain relevance
summary: TRACE is a reinforcement learning framework that allocates rollout budgets across tree-structured multi-turn agent trajectories to improve reward contrast during LLM training.

killReason: off-domain security research — no creative-relevant AI angle (image, video, design, generation)
summary: A paper revealing that grammar-constrained decoding can be exploited to jailbreak LLMs into generating malicious code, and proposing a defense called CodeShield.

killReason: technically interesting but too narrowly focused on egocentric video spatial reasoning benchmarks — not a notable creative-relevant advance in image/video generation, design, or visual output that a creative would care to know about
summary: ReRe is a training-free inference framework that improves spatial reasoning in egocentric videos by having a multimodal LLM form a hypothesis from the original video, then revise it using a synthesized novel-view video rendered from predicted 3D geometry.

killReason: pure infrastructure/architecture research with no creative-relevant application — MoE router optimization is LLM training internals, not image/video/3D/design generation or any creative domain
summary: A paper proposing Manifold Power Iteration (MPI) to redesign MoE router matrices by aligning router rows with principal singular directions of associated experts, improving routing efficiency in 1B–11B parameter language models.

killReason: Off-domain — coding agent benchmarking has no relevance to creative work (design, image, video, motion, photography, 3D)
summary: Claw-SWE-Bench introduces a multilingual benchmark and adapter protocol for evaluating general-purpose AI agent harnesses on GitHub issue-resolution coding tasks across 8 languages and 43 repositories.

killReason: genuinely off-domain — combinatorics math benchmarking for LLMs has no relevance to creative work in image, video, design, or any discipline this audience works in
summary: ComBench is an Olympiad-level combinatorics benchmark with 100 annotated problems designed to evaluate and compare large language models on proof reasoning and constructive realization tasks.

killReason: not creative-domain relevant — focused on autonomous ML research optimization, not image/video/design/3D generation or any creative discipline
summary: Arbor is a research framework where an AI agent autonomously runs iterative hypothesis testing and experimentation across long-horizon ML research tasks using a persistent hypothesis tree structure.

killReason: genuinely off-domain — tabular data benchmarking is pure ML infrastructure with no connection to creative-relevant AI (image, video, 3D, design, generation)
summary: TRL-Bench is a standardized benchmark for evaluating and comparing tabular data encoders across different training paradigms using shared downstream evaluation conditions.

killReason: Reward modeling infrastructure paper — technically creative-adjacent (text-to-image post-training) but too deep in ML plumbing (reward model architecture, policy-gradient training) to be a meaningful reference point for working creatives; not a notable lab release and the advance is narrow enough to fall below the Papers bar for this audience.
summary: Z-Reward is a teacher-student reward modeling framework for text-to-image post-training that represents visual preferences as score distributions rather than scalar values, improving human preference alignment accuracy while remaining efficient to deploy.

killReason: genuinely off-domain — agent environment engineering is pure ML infrastructure with no creative-workflow relevance
summary: A survey paper systematically categorizing research on environments for LLM-based agents, covering environment modeling, synthesis, evaluation, and agent-environment co-evolution paradigms.

killReason: technically notable but too infrastructure-focused — long-video benchmark gains and KV-cache optimization are off-domain from creative work; no clear relevance to image, video generation, design, or creative production workflows
summary: InternVideo3 is a research framework for long-horizon video understanding that uses closed-loop multimodal reasoning and a new attention mechanism to improve performance on video comprehension benchmarks.

killReason: pure RL training infrastructure — optimizing LLM rollout throughput has no creative-domain relevance
summary: Bebop is a research paper presenting methods to improve multi-token prediction acceptance rates during reinforcement learning training of large language models, achieving up to 1.8x end-to-end speedup on Qwen models.

killReason: robotics/manipulation research, not creative-relevant AI
summary: World Pilot is a robotics research framework that augments Vision-Language-Action models with world-model priors to improve robot manipulation performance on out-of-distribution tasks.

killReason: pure software engineering / code-agent infrastructure, not creative-relevant AI (image, video, 3D, design, generation)

killReason: pure ML interpretability infrastructure — off-domain from creative work (image, video, design, generation)

killReason: LLM training methodology — reinforcement learning optimization for reasoning models — has no creative-relevant application; off-domain for designers, photographers, or motion work.
